name: Embalse de García Sola y Casas de Don Pedro — circular en BTT por Puebla de Alcocer
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Talarrubias are characterized by the "Appalachian relief" of Extremadura, featuring alternating valleys and mountain ranges. The region includes several sierras like Sierra de Siruela and Sierra del Aljibe, with elevations ranging from 300 to 940 meters, offering varied slopes and technical terrain. Expansive water bodies such as the Zújar and La Serena reservoirs also shape the landscape, providing scenic paths. This diverse environment, including ancient oak forests known as "dehesas," creates a wide array of mountain biking opportunities. Best mountain bike trails around Talarrubias The most popular mountain bike trail is Puebla de Alcocer to Cerro Masatrigo and Embalse de la Serena Loop, a 31.1 miles (50.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes and views of the Embalse de la Serena. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de García Sola y Casas de Don Pedro — circular en BTT por Puebla de Alcocer, a moderate 21.0 miles (33.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views around the reservoir and through the local countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Herrera del Duque Castle Loop Trail, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the historic castle, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Talarrubias is defined by its "Appalachian relief," numerous sierras, and large reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Talarrubias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Talarrubias?
A: You'll find 18 mountain bike trails around Talarrubias. These include 2 easy routes, 13 moderate options, and 3 challenging trails for experienced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Talarrubias?
A: The region around Talarrubias features a distinct 'Appalachian relief' with alternating valleys and mountain ranges like the Sierra de Siruela and Sierra del Aljibe. This means you can expect varied slopes, from gentle paths along the Zújar and La Serena reservoirs to more technical climbs in the sierras. The landscape also includes ancient oak forests known as 'dehesas'.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Talarrubias?
A: While many trails in Talarrubias are moderate to difficult, there are 2 routes specifically rated as easy. These are ideal for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride through the scenic landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Talarrubias?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Talarrubias are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201072706/embalse-de-garcia-sola-y-casas-de-don-pedro-circular-en-btt-por-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de García Sola y Casas de Don Pedro — circular en BTT por Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop around the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often offer views of the expansive Zújar and La Serena reservoirs. You might also encounter landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, a distinctive mountain, or the impressive rock formations at Puerto Peña, known for its griffon vulture colony. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7576749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de las Dehesas – Guadiana Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; also provides scenic sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Talarrubias?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Talarrubias, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are typically mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201089160/puebla-de-alcocer-to-cerro-masatrigo-and-embalse-de-la-serena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Alcocer to Cerro Masatrigo and Embalse de la Serena Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover over 50 km with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes combine natural beauty with historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201103126/herrera-del-duque-castle-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Castle Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through varied terrain around a historic castle, offering both a ride and a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Talarrubias?
A: Mountain bikers often praise the diverse landscape, from the challenging 'Appalachian relief' to the serene reservoir views. The region's rich natural environment, including the dehesas and opportunities for birdwatching, also adds to the appeal, offering a unique blend of sport and nature.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The Zújar and La Serena reservoirs are prominent features of the landscape. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201072706/embalse-de-garcia-sola-y-casas-de-don-pedro-circular-en-btt-por-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de García Sola y Casas de Don Pedro — circular en BTT por Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; offer continuous scenic views along the water's edge.

Q: Where can I find information about parking for mountain biking in Talarrubias?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every route, many trails start from towns or villages like Puebla de Alcocer or Herrera del Duque, where public parking is generally available. It's advisable to check local information for specific starting points of your chosen route.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes through natural reserves?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Nature Reserve – Herrera del Duque Town Center loop from Herrera del Duque&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride through the Cíjara Nature Reserve, allowing you to experience its protected landscapes.
tips: A fairly flat section that follows the flow of the channel.

The urban complex of Casas de Don Pedro is based on popular architecture that is evident in the different ancestral and stately houses that make up the town, with elements present in them such as grilles or noble shields. There are also some notable monuments in the urban center, such as the sculpture “Help the fallen” by Juan de Ávalos. In the main square we find the town hall, with a portico, and near it the Plaza del Sagrado Corazón, in which the church of San Pedro Apóstol is located. Both squares would once be the administrative center and the religious center of the population, respectively.

waypoints.name: Church of San Pedro Apóstol, Casas de Don Pedro
